newsSinceJun6: No company-specific fundamental news. The -7.0% June 9 drop (after -6.9% on June 5, with intraday prints as low as EUR 87.35, -13%) is part of a global post-Computex profit-taking wave in AI semiconductor names hitting German chip suppliers (Aixtron, Suss Microtec, Wacker also down). Stock is now ~18% below its early-June annual high of EUR 108.60. Standing overhangs predate June 6: Wacker Chemie placed ~6% of share capital on May 26; UBS withdrew its buy rating calling the stock fairly valued at EUR 104; AlphaValue cut 2026 EPS on supply-chain inventory burdens; consensus price target (~EUR 81) sits below spot even after the pullback. Q1 2026 was weak (revenue -17.5% q/q) and FY26 guidance calls for sales mid-single-digit % below 2025, with AI/300mm the only firm demand pocket.

verdicts.rankRationale: Flow-driven post-Computex flush (-18% off the June high) modestly improves entry asymmetry, but on this bottleneck-quality lens nothing changed: wafers outside AI/300mm remain in inventory overhang, Q1 missed, and consensus PT (~EUR 81) is still below spot, so the hold from June 8 stands with slight positive drift.
verdicts.bull: AI/HBM is structurally shifting demand to 300mm wafers where Siltronic is one of only ~5 global suppliers; an 18% drawdown on zero new fundamental news rebuilds upside into the eventual non-AI inventory normalization.
verdicts.neutral: Stock is still +75% YTD against a FY26 guide for declining sales; the sell-off merely unwinds May's +31% froth back toward UBS's EUR 104 'fairly valued' and consensus EUR 81, leaving rank 43 about right.
verdicts.bear: Q1 revenue fell 17.5% q/q, non-AI end markets (auto, industrial, smartphones) stay soft, price pressure persists outside LTAs, and Wacker's residual ~25% stake is a recurring placement overhang on any rally.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results / guidance update showing 300mm AI-driven wafer orders outpacing the inventory drawdown, or a new LTA/capacity commitment from an HBM customer.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Inventory overhang outside AI persists into 2027 and pricing pressure compounds, making the +75% YTD re-rating unwind fundamental rather than flow-driven.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down call on this crowding-sensitive list was driven by the +90% YTD chase; that exact risk has now partially discharged via a flow-driven -18% flush from the high with the upstream wafer-substrate thesis intact, which on this page's own discipline improves residual asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: Sharp sell-off with no thesis-relevant news is the textbook asymmetry-restoring setup for an upstream substrate bottleneck name; 300mm AI demand shift is unchanged and the momentum crowd is being washed out.
verdicts.neutral: Crowding has eased but not cleared - still +75% YTD on a EUR 3.5B cap with consensus PT below price, so the move up should be modest, not a re-tiering.
verdicts.bear: The 'bottleneck' is not currently binding: wafer supply outside AI is in oversupply with customers destocking, so a scaling-driven order inflection could be quarters away while guidance points to lower 2026 sales.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence that AI fab and HBM capacity additions are tightening 300mm wafer supply (LTA renewals, pricing stabilization, or order-intake commentary at Q2).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-Computex AI de-rating deepens across the sector and momentum unwind overshoots, dragging the name down regardless of the substrate thesis.
